channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: North Korea launches missiles toward eastern waters in 2nd launch of year, South says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Tuesday test-fired multiple missiles toward its eastern waters, South Korea’s military said, as it continued its weapons demonstrations ahead of Donald Trump’s return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canada ready to buy more American products to appease Trump's tariff threat, ambassador says
item.description: PALM DESERT, Calif. (AP) — Canada's ambassador to Washington said Monday that Canada is prepared to buy more from the United States, including its next fleet of submarines, to appease President-elect Donald Trump's concerns about the U.S.-Canada trade deficit.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cease-fire between Israel and Hamas getting closer amid concerns terror group rearming in Gaza
item.description: Israel and Hamas are nearing a cease-fire deal amid escalating death toll; 5 IDF soldiers were killed in Gaza on Monday amid growing warnings that Hamas is rearming.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: Brazil’s President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations.
item.dc:creator: Mauricio Savarese And Diarlei Rodrigues, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At least 100 illegal miners have died while trapped in a South African mine for months, group says
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — At least 100 men who were mining illegally in an abandoned gold mine in South Africa have died of suspected starvation and dehydration after being trapped deep underground for months while police tried to force them out, a group representing the miners said Monday.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jake Sullivan: 'We are so close' to ceasefire, hostage deal in Gaza
item.description: White House national security adviser Jake Sullivan said Monday the U.S. is “so close to a ceasefire and hostage deal” in Gaza, as President Biden races to secure an agreement on a major foreign policy objective before leaving office in a week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Watch: White House holds press briefing as latest Israel-Hamas ceasefire emerges
item.description: The White House held a press briefing on Monday in the midst of another ceasefire deal emerging between Israel and Hamas as the war in Gaza rages on. Several sticking points remain as officials work to secure an end to more than one year of fighting in the coastal enclave.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Former ambassador to Denmark: NATO would be compelled to respond to Greenland invasion
item.description: Rufus Gifford, a former U.S. ambassador to Denmark, said in a Sunday interview that the NATO alliance would be compelled to respond to any invasion or incursion into Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Head of the International Court of Justice is named Lebanon's new prime minister
item.description: Lebanon’s new president has asked prominent diplomat and jurist Nawaf Salam to form the country’s new government after Salam was named prime minister by a large number of legislators.
item.dc:creator: By Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Baby gorilla finds home at Istanbul zoo after rescue
item.description: Five-month-old Zeytin is recovering at an Istanbul zoo after being rescued from a cargo hold on a Turkish Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: World's largest religious gathering begins in India, hundreds of millions of Hindus expected to attend
item.description: The Maha Kumbh Mela, or Hindu pitcher festival, begins Monday in the northern Indian city of Prayagraj. Officials expect at least 400 million attendees over the next 45 days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Significant progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: Officials say U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal has not been reached yet.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Eastwood, Samy Magdy And Josef Federman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden says he's leaving Trump with a 'strong hand to play' in world conflicts
item.description: As President Joe Biden prepares to leave office next week, he's insistent that his one-term presidency has made strides in restoring American credibility on the world stage and has proved that the U.S. remains an indispensable partner around the globe.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Croatia's President Milanović wins another term after defeating ruling party candidate in runoff
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia's opposition-backed President Zoran Milanović, a critic of the European Union and NATO, overwhelmingly won reelection for another five-year term on Sunday, defeating a candidate from the ruling conservative party in a runoff vote, official results showed.
item.dc:creator: SAŠA KAVIĆ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU ministers will consider easing sanctions on Syria at a meeting later in January
item.description: RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — European Union foreign ministers will meet in late January to discuss easing sanctions imposed on Syria, the bloc's foreign policy chief said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BARAA ANWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's Trudeau urges US consumers to consider the harm of Trump's tariff threats
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Canada's outgoing Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Sunday suggested that President-elect Donald Trump's remarks about Canada becoming America's “51st state” has distracted attention from the harm that steep tariffs would inflict on U.S. consumers.
item.dc:creator: JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No pants? No problem. London subway riders bare their legs to brighten a gray winter day
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Mind the pants. Hundreds of Londoners headed down to the Underground on Sunday afternoon, stripped down to their underwear and travelled around a bit, trying to look as though nothing unusual was going on. As if.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's talk of expansion puts world leaders on alert
item.description: President-elect Trump’s talk of territorial expansion has rattled world leaders at an already precarious time in global politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump setting up meeting with Putin, in communication with Xi
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said he is working on setting up a meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin for after his Jan. 20 inauguration and is in "communication" with Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lebanon's new president strikes a nationalistic tone amid regional shifts, further weakening of Hezbollah
item.description: The Lebanese parliament finally voted on a new president on Thursday following two years of deadlock. Joseph Aoun the head of the country's armed forces won a majority of the votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Details of Venezuelan opposition leader's possible arrest remain unclear amid Maduro inauguration resistance
item.description: Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado's aides claim she was detained Thursday, despite denials by the country's socialist government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia monitoring Trump’s ‘dramatic’ comments on Greenland acquisition
item.description: The Kremlin said Thursday they are monitoring President-elect Donald Trump's "dramatic" comments over his desire to acquire Greenland amid other expansionist remarks, including the Panama Canal and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The historic importance of Greenland for US national security as debate over island's future roars on
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's recent comments on Greenland has put the world's largest island, a district of Denmark, to the top of many foreign policy discussions. Experts weigh in on its importance to US national security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cold spell claims first two lives in El Paso-Juarez area
item.description: Tuesday’s death of a homeless man presumably due to hypothermia has set off alarms in a border city with a large floating population of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation
